title: Rare Evo and Dev Gov Day 2026: Cardano Title Sponsorship

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Why: Pinned anchor document is available for this action. Additional context: Treasury withdrawal actions require elevated scrutiny. Confidence context: score -0.66; confidence 100.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Doctrine-aware reasoning layer nudged the score by -0.030 (clamped to ±0.05): The deterministic assessment is blocked by missing budget, feasibility, alternatives, failure-mode, and community-impact analysis, and the extracted claims include a large treasury request, mutable sponsorship allocation, and a VIP revenue return that is asserted without enough quantified downside protection. Under the doctrine, public-benefit ecosystem growth does not outweigh treasury stewardship and evidence quality when spending justification is incomplete.
top_fixes: Use stable, reproducible anchor links (commit-pinned when possible).
Add line-item budget with milestone-gated disbursement and objective completion checks.
Document downside risks, mitigations, and what gets cut first if delivery slips.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Cardano title sponsorship for Rare Evo 2026 main stage and global livestream plus title sponsorship/support for Rare Dev Gov Day 2026.

Deliverables: Cardano title sponsorship of Rare Evo 2026 main stage and livestream, Support for Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026 on July 28, 2026 at ARIA Las Vegas, Free General Admission access for the Cardano community, Complimentary booth space for selected Cardano ecosystem startup projects, Five Cardano main stage panelist positions, Breakout room and workshop space, Travel and lodging support for 20 Cardano community members, Livestream Cardano ads and media coverage, Return of 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury
Deadline/expiry: Rare Dev Gov Day on July 28, 2026 and Rare Evo 2026 on July 28-31, 2026
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, described as $660,000 at a $0.24 ADA valuation, to fund two Cardano sponsorship packages.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The requested budget is broken into ₳1,166,666.67 for Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026, ₳833,333.33 for Rare Evo 2026 Cardano title sponsorship, and ₳750,000 for Intersect, audit, fees, tax withholdings, and a Dev Gov Day 2027 deposit.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Rare Network says it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with the current retail value of a VIP ticket stated as $900.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Rare Evo 2026 will occur regardless of treasury funding, but the proposal says treasury funding would expand Cardano presence, accessibility, participation, and strategic industry outreach.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Rare Network claims it has been active in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020 and has operated Rare Evo annually for five consecutive years.

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal reports Rare Evo 2025 metrics including 2,500 attendees, 80 exhibitor booths, 85 speakers, 100+ ecosystem partners, 20K+ livestream views, 4M social media impressions, and 40+ Cardano projects represented.
missing: Independent evidence for: Rare Network says it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with the current retail value of a VIP ticket stated as $900.

Independent evidence for: Rare Evo 2026 will occur regardless of treasury funding, but the proposal says treasury funding would expand Cardano presence, accessibility, participation, and strategic industry outreach.
Independent evidence for: Rare Network claims it has been active in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020 and has operated Rare Evo annually for five consecutive years.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states sponsorship allocations may change if another sponsor purchases the mainstage or livestream package before approval, with Cardano receiving sponsorship of equal impact or dollar value instead.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, described as $660,000 at a $0.24 ADA valuation, to fund two Cardano sponsorship packages." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Rare Network says it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with the current retail value of a VIP ticket stated as $900." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
